beta.demoPartnersBanner: Partners marked DEMO are placeholder data for the beta so the marketplace strip is not empty. They are not real stores — do not expect real purchases or shipping.
beta.demoFeedBanner: Posts with a DEMO badge are seeded sample content so the community feed is not empty during the beta. Your own posts are real.
beta.agreementIntro: You are joining the closed beta. Please confirm the following so we can keep feedback structured and protect both you and the product.
beta.agreementBullet1: You will use pre-release software that may change, break, or lose data; keep backups of anything important outside the app.
beta.agreementBullet2: You agree to send honest feedback (bugs, confusion, ideas) through the in-app bug reporter or by replying to team emails.
beta.agreementBullet3: You will not hold TarantulApp liable for issues arising from beta use; support may be limited and features may be reset.
beta.agreementBullet4: You may be contacted about the beta (missions, surveys). You can leave the beta program by contacting support.
beta.agreementCheckbox: I have read and agree to participate under these conditions.
beta.agreementNeedCheckbox: Please confirm the checkbox to continue.
beta.agreementError: Could not save your acceptance. Try again.

social.metaDescription: Feed, spoods, and invites: a private space for signed-in keepers.
social.tagline: The online hangout for keepers who actually live the tub life.
social.lead: Share spider photos, molts, and milestones. Each post has its own visibility — separate from selling on the marketplace.
social.spoodTeaser: Spood: that's what we call community likes.
social.shareHint: Invite friends from the Invite tab.
social.carouselHint: Use Sex ID for voting, or open public discussions from this carousel.
social.loadError: Could not load. Check your connection and try again.
social.newPostCollapsedHint: Ideas rotate slowly while you browse — open when you’re ready to write.
social.composerBodyPh: Ask away: what is on your mind? Add a photo, a question, or a win.
social.postMediaLabel: Photo or video (image max 8MB / video max 35MB)
social.promptBeginner1: Ask away: not sure if it is pre-molt fasting? Say what you are seeing — photos help a lot.
social.promptBeginner2: Ask away: is your first setup’s airflow and substrate balance in a good place? Other keepers will weigh in.
social.promptBeginner3: Ask away: stuck on humidity or watering? Describe the tub and the room — people love to compare notes.
social.promptBeginner4: Ask away: odd or normal behavior? A short story plus a picture usually gets the best feedback.
social.promptGeneral1: Ask away: what small enclosure change made the biggest difference for you this week?
social.promptGeneral2: Share or ask: a recent molt, feeding, or growth moment — brag, troubleshoot, or both.
social.promptGeneral3: Ask away: what would you redo in your setup today? Your hindsight helps the next person.
social.promptGeneral4: Ask away — any hobby question. Someone here has already tried it (or failed and learned).
social.spoodPickThread: Pick a thread or start a new one.
social.inviteLead: Share your link: short Pro trial extensions plus milestone bonuses as friends actually sign up.
social.referralKeyline: Invite other keepers — improve your results.
social.referralLadderIntro: Milestone ladder (one-time bonus when you cross each tier, on top of +3/+3 per signup):
social.inviteBonusLine: +{{referee}} days for your friend on signup, +{{referrer}} days for you when they use your code.
social.referralHeroSub: Your friend gets trial days on signup; you stack short bonuses per invite plus one-time milestone extras.
social.referralProgressHint: Larger one-time bonuses unlock at the ladder thresholds (on top of the per-signup days below).
social.referralLadderIntroShort: Milestone rows reflect your confirmed invites and claimed ladder bonuses from the server.
social.inviteShareMessage: I organize my tarantulas, molts, feedings, and enclosures in TarantulApp. It's free, includes free QR labels for each T, and we both get perks if you join with my link: {{link}}
social.communityOpenBanner: Open community: you can read posts. To publish, comment, or vote, please sign in.
social.noSpoodYet: This post has no Spood yet.
social.threadViewHint: Thread view keeps the conversation focused. Jump back to Community for broader browsing.

sexIdCase.authorNoVote: You created this case — you can’t vote on your own post.
sexIdCase.loginToVote: Sign in to add your vote and keep one vote per case.
sexIdCase.shareCta: Share the case (invite with your referral on the link)
sexIdCase.refHint: Get your code on Community → Invite to add ?ref= to the share link.
sexIdCase.formHint: Upload a clear photo. Friends vote once; you can’t vote on your own case.
sexIdCase.fieldTitlePh: e.g. Which side are you on?

about.metaDescription: TarantulApp™: a serious logbook for your spiders, sourced taxonomy (WSC/GBIF), terrarium QR labels, and a community marketplace.
about.title: About TarantulApp™
about.tagline: Method in the rack. Names you can defend.
about.lead: We built the app we wanted above our enclosures: less flex, more traceability. Your daily notes, source-backed sheets, marketplace, and community already live together here in one keeper flow: the place our hobby needs and deserves.
about.section1Body: Paper logs are fine until a molt date walks away from you. We pair the real pace of each tub with a calm UI: what it ate, when it molted, how the enclosure looks — plus optional public pages so you can teach without leaking what you want private.
about.section2Body: We wire in the World Spider Catalog (via GBIF) and the GBIF backbone for Theraphosidae. Care text and catalog photos blend our curation with public observations (iNaturalist) or GBIF media when they exist. When literature moves a name, you will see it reflected; if something smells off, open GBIF and verify — we are not rewriting science in a back room.
about.section3Body: Your private inventory keeps a care timeline, reminders, and per-enclosure QR codes for quick scans. Turn on a public specimen card, activate your keeper profile, and share updates in community to learn faster, compare husbandry, and contribute real context back to the hobby.
about.section4Title: Marketplace and community: corkboard, not a cash register
about.section4Body: Marketplace and community connect inside the same app, so you move from records to conversation to direct deals without leaving your keeper flow. We do not run payments or sit in the middle of your deal with another keeper. Official partners are human-reviewed and link out to their own sites; laws, permits, biosecurity, and animal welfare stay on you.
about.section5Title: Privacy and what we are not
about.section5Body: We are not a livestock shop or a substitute for a licensed exotic vet in your jurisdiction. Data is governed by our privacy policy and terms; export or deletion lives in Account. Found a bug or an idea? Email us — humans read it.
about.closing: If the vibe fits, browse Discover without an account, add Pro when the collection outgrows the basics, and tell us what is missing — TarantulApp™ is being cooked with you.

discover.title: Discover species
discover.intro: Browse official taxonomy (World Spider Catalog via GBIF) and the in-app catalog with filters. Photos come from iNaturalist or GBIF media when available.
discover.introTaxonomyOnly: Search the official spider checklist (WSC via GBIF + GBIF backbone in Theraphosidae). Tap a result to see a care sheet when we have one in our public catalog — otherwise we show taxonomy only and point you to GBIF.
discover.noSearchHits: No matches in our public catalog, WSC, or GBIF (Theraphosidae) for that fragment.
discover.dataDisclaimer: Taxonomy: World Spider Catalog via GBIF where applicable; broader search in Theraphosidae (GBIF). Images: iNaturalist or GBIF species media when data exists.
discover.taxonNotFound: We could not find that taxon in GBIF.
discover.speciesNotFound: Species is not available in the public catalog.
discover.noPhoto: No automatic reference photo for this taxon.
discover.compareNeedParams: Open compare from a species sheet or add URL parameters (e.g. ?a=1&b=2 or ?gbifA=…&gbifB=…).
discover.comparePickLocalPrompt: Pick the second species from the local catalog:
discover.compareProBody: With Pro or your free trial you can view two sheets side by side to compare size, level, humidity, and more.
discover.openDedicatedSheet: Open this sheet on its own page (stable URL)
discover.openDedicatedTaxon: Open this taxon on its own page (stable URL)
discover.loginCtaBody: Create an account to log feedings, molts, and reminders. Meanwhile you can explore species using official data (WSC/GBIF) and iNaturalist photos — no sign-in required.
discover.loginToAddHint: Sign in or create an account to add this species to your collection (the form will be prefilled).
discover.loginOrRegisterToAdd: Sign in or register to add
discover.addGuestCtaBody: Sign in or create an account to add this species — the add form opens with species already selected. You can also sign in just to open your collection.
discover.addLimitReached: Your Free plan is limited to 6 spiders. Upgrade to Pro or start a trial to add more.
discover.specimenMaxHint: Maximum for this estimate: {{max}} cm (species adult max + 10%).
discover.catalogLoadError: Could not load the catalog. Make sure the API is running and check the browser network tab for CORS or 500 errors.
discover.profileFootnote: Care parameters reflect keeper literature and community guides where available. Taxonomic data from GBIF / WSC.
discover.noKeeperSheetTitle: No care sheet in TarantulApp™ yet
discover.noKeeperSheetBody: Taxonomy here comes from GBIF / WSC — that’s authoritative for names, but it doesn’t include husbandry. Our species cards are built from a separate catalog we curate; this name isn’t linked to a sheet yet (or GBIF didn’t return a preview). It’s not a bug on your side.
discover.noKeeperSheetBodyShort: Taxonomy is from GBIF / WSC. We don’t have a linked keeper-care sheet for this taxon in our public catalog yet — that’s normal for many names. You can still open GBIF or add the spider and import it from there.
discover.taxonomySourceNote: Name and rank shown follow {{source}} / GBIF. Husbandry in TarantulApp™ only appears when this taxon is linked to our curated catalog entry.
discover.browseIntro: Type at least two characters: results load as you type from our public species catalog, then WSC and GBIF (Theraphosidae). Pick a catalog row for the full care profile; otherwise we show taxonomy-only when GBIF has no linked sheet.
discover.marketplaceHubBody: Use Discover to research a species, then head to the marketplace to buy or trade with other keepers—or post your own listing. We do not process payments or broker your deal.
discover.marketplaceHubOfficial: We also highlight certified partners—brands our team has reviewed, with links to shop on their own sites.
discover.terrariumSizeHint: Optional — a rough terrarium footprint from your spider’s body length (same heuristic as the collection view). Not a substitute for measuring your animal and your own research.
discover.pageFooterDisclaimer: Care parameters and hobby notes (including New vs Old World) come from TarantulApp™’s curated public catalog and keeper-oriented text where present; they are inferred or generalized when noted. Taxonomic names and links use GBIF / WSC. Reference photos may come from iNaturalist or other open data. Nothing here is veterinary or legal advice.
discover.speciesProfileTaxonomyShort: Taxonomy ({{source}}) — no keeper care block in the app catalog yet.
discover.speciesProfileTaxonomyApology: Taxonomy from GBIF/WSC only. Our public species catalog doesn’t link a care sheet for this name yet — that’s normal, not a problem on your side.
discover.seoHubTitle: Tarantula species catalog: care sheets & taxonomy | TarantulApp™
discover.seoHubDescription: Search by scientific name: husbandry sheets (habitat, size, humidity, substrate), official WSC/GBIF taxonomy, and reference photos. Free without signing in.
discover.seoSpeciesDescription: {{name}} care sheet{{common}} on TarantulApp™: {{meta}} Origin, humidity, ventilation, and temperament when listed in our public catalog. Taxonomy from GBIF / WSC.
discover.seoSpeciesLead: Public keeper-focused parameters from our curated catalog, with taxonomy aligned to GBIF and the World Spider Catalog. Not veterinary advice.
discover.seoTaxonDescription: {{name}} taxon page in Discover (GBIF): official name, reference image when available, and link to GBIF. Detailed husbandry blocks appear when the taxon is linked to TarantulApp™’s local catalog.
discover.seoCompareDescription: Compare two catalog sheets or two GBIF taxa (Pro or trial): size, level, humidity, and more side by side.
discover.exploreNewWorldHint: Americas — hobby sheets tagged New World
discover.exploreOldWorldHint: Africa, Asia, Oceania — tagged Old World
discover.exploreSemiArborealHint: Often climbs; starts with arboreal browse
discover.catalogBannerBody: Continuously updated from our public catalog, GBIF, and the World Spider Catalog (Theraphosidae) where linked.
discover.browseEmpty: No rows match these filters yet.

qrTool.metaDescription: Generate a QR from your live collection spiders for enclosure labels. The public link is inside the QR; download a clean print-ready PNG.
qrTool.heading: QR labels for your enclosures
qrTool.intro: Pick a spider from your collection: the QR opens its public profile. The first live entry is selected by default; if you land here right after adding one, that spider is chosen.
qrTool.previewPlaceholder: Sign in and add at least one live spider to your collection to see the code.
qrTool.loginRequiredBody: To generate and print a QR you need an account and at least one live spider in your collection.
qrTool.emptyCollection: You have no live spiders in your collection yet. Add one to generate its QR.
qrTool.footerNote: The PNG contains only the QR and a small “TarantulApp™” mark. Name and URL are not repeated on the image—they’re already in the QR and on the profile.
qrTool.schemaDescription: Free tool to create QR codes from your tarantula collection, linking each spider’s public profile.

publicBetaHome.tagline: Built for keepers who log the enclosure, not the hype.
publicBetaHome.lead: Access to the full app is invite-only during beta. Sign in if we created your tester account, or apply to join the beta.

betaPending.title: Beta access required
betaPending.lead: Your account is signed in, but it doesn’t have beta tester access yet. Apply below and we’ll review your request.
betaPending.footerHint: If we already invited you, ask us to mark your email as a tester — new accounts are created by the team during beta.

auth.standardManifestLead: This is where enclosure work meets the details you actually read: one app for official taxonomy, your logbook, and species cards with the fields you open when you feed, molt-check, or dial humidity—grounded in recognized catalogs (WSC, GBIF) and filled with keeper-grade signal, not fluff.
auth.standardManifestB1: Cards built on serious checklists and kept focused on what matters: habitat, humidity, temperament, substrate, ventilation—and provenance back to the source when it matters.
auth.standardManifestB2: Collection control: feedings, molts, behavior, and reminders built around the real pace of each enclosure.
auth.standardManifestB3: Labeling and traceability: terrarium QR codes, side-by-side species comparison, and exports for people who document everything.
auth.standardManifestB4: Small circle, high bar: built for keepers who would rather log, measure, and close the lid with intent.
auth.standardCircleKicker: Not another hobby feed—your enclosure lab, with serious names and a logbook that ages well.
auth.standardManifestAboutHint: short manifest: promise and honest boundaries.
auth.invalidResetToken: This link is invalid or expired. Request a new one from “Forgot password”.
auth.registrationClosed: Public sign-up is closed. Accounts are created by the team for beta testers; use Apply for beta or sign in with the email we provisioned.
auth.betaLoginSubtitle: Sign in with the beta account we created for you.
auth.loginSubtitle: Open your collection and the logbook behind it
auth.registerSubtitle: Create your account and start tracking
auth.name: Name
auth.registerKeeperHint: We assign your public @keeper handle automatically from your name or email so you can use the marketplace and social features right away. You can change it later in Account settings, where we also show if a handle is available.
auth.referralCodePlaceholder: If someone invited you, paste their code
auth.resetSent: Done! If that email exists, you'll receive a link in your inbox.
auth.resetSuccess: Password updated. You can now sign in.
auth.passwordTooShort: Password must be at least 6 characters.
auth.trialRegisterPromo: New accounts get a 7-day free trial with full Pro access. After that: stay on Free (Cases, Community, Discover, basic AI) or move to Pro ($4.99/mo) for full logs, exports, and bulk QR. Referrals add short trial days and milestones — invite other keepers and improve your results.
auth.proTeaserBody: Pro ($4.99/mo): unlimited spiders, full history, exports (Excel, PDF), bulk QR, deeper AI, and full Case analytics. Pro+ and Vendor roll out next; Free stays the wide funnel with Cases, Community, and Discover.
auth.googleLoginError: Could not sign in with Google.
auth.googlePendingConfig: Google login is visible, pending client ID configuration.

loginPage.heroTagline: Your collection, community, and marketplace in one flow.
loginPage.heroLead: No visual noise or filler screens—just real tools: official Discover, day-to-day specimen care, terrarium QR, a public community, and a commercial layer that scales with you.
loginPage.featureDiscoverB2: Side-by-side species comparison for keeper decisions (Pro).
loginPage.featureCollectionB1: Feeding, molt, and behavior logs per specimen.
loginPage.featureCollectionB2: Single-spider QR and bulk Word QR for enclosures (Pro).
loginPage.featureMarketplaceB2: Certified partners and a keeper profile with reputation.
loginPage.featureCommunityB1: A public feed so anyone can see the value up front.
loginPage.featureCommunityB2: Signed in: post, comment, and vote on Sex ID and more.
loginPage.previewLead: The community is public: anyone can read posts before creating an account.

common.error: Something went wrong. Please try again.

marketplace.metaDescription: Community listings, official partners, and breeder profiles in MX, US, and Canada. TarantulApp™ is not a middleman: keepers connect directly.
marketplace.title: Keeper marketplace
marketplace.communityIntro: Keepers post listings for sale, swaps, or whatever you agree privately. TarantulApp™ is not a middleman: we do not take a cut, verify identities in person, or guarantee shipments. While the board fills across Mexico, the United States, and Canada, demo listings in major metros preview the full flow.
marketplace.communityDisclaimer: Meet in safe places when possible, keep chat receipts, check local wildlife/shipping rules, and avoid risky prepayments. If something looks off, use Report or contact us.
marketplace.officialStripSub: Shops and breeders reviewed by our team, linking straight to their site. The marketplace is still mostly community-driven — this is an optional shortcut when you want a vetted storefront.
marketplace.officialApplyToggle: Shop or breeder? Request official status
marketplace.searchPlaceholder: Search by species or listing title
marketplace.officialSubtitle: Trusted shops and breeders to start or grow your collection. Buy on their official site.
marketplace.officialEmpty: No official partners match these filters yet.
marketplace.officialApplyTitle: Breeder or shop and want to be listed here?
marketplace.officialApplyBlurb: Tell us about your brand; our team reviews every request before it goes live.
marketplace.officialLeadSent: Thanks — we will review your official partner request.
marketplace.messagingCardSub: The messages you’ve exchanged on the marketplace.

insights.title: Insights
insights.lead: Collection activity based on feeding and molt logs (successful feeds only — refused meals are excluded from charts).
insights.feedingAttentionHint: Shows living spiders sorted by longest gap since last successful feed (never-fed first). Open a spider and log feeding from Timeline.
insights.feedingAttentionEmpty: No active spiders in your collection — add one or restore from history.

dashboard.keeperAtmosphereStrip: Your spiders and molt cadence run the show here—not the timeline. Marketplace and community are now part of the same home flow.
dashboard.title: My Tarantulas
dashboard.emptyDesc: Add your first tarantula to start their file.
dashboard.noResults: No results for the selected filters.
dashboard.limitReached: You've reached the free plan limit of 6 tarantulas. Upgrade to Pro to add more.
dashboard.readOnlyHint: Read-only for this spider on the free plan — upgrade to Pro or delete spiders to unlock editing.
dashboard.trialBanner: Your free trial is active with full Pro access.
dashboard.qrBannerLead: Label your tubs with a free QR — link each enclosure to its spider.
dashboard.qrBulkPrintTitle: Print QR codes for multiple spiders (.docx)

qrBulk.pageSubtitle: Pick specimens and download a Word file ready for labels or shows.
qrBulk.proOnlyBody: Download a Word file with multiple codes: fixed-size grid or a flexible layout you can tweak in Word. Subscribe to Pro or use the 7-day trial.
qrBulk.empty: No spiders to show with the current filters.
qrBulk.exportHint: Each QR opens the same public profile as in the app (/t/…). If a spider is private, visitors only see data when logged in as you, or after you enable a public card (Pro).
qrBulk.sizeHelp: Every QR in the “fixed size” file uses this measurement—handy for identical labels.
qrBulk.trimWarning: Only the first {{max}} selected spiders are included in the file.
qrBulk.docFooterNote: Codes link to each specimen’s public card. In Word: File → Print.
qrBulk.docFooterNoteFlex: Smaller QR blocks with more whitespace—resize, duplicate, or rearrange in Word before printing.

readOnly.banner: Your trial has ended. You can still browse your collection and history. Subscribe to Pro to add or edit entries — and to export your collection to Excel.
readOnly.overLimitBanner: On the free plan you can edit your 6 oldest spiders; any extra spiders are read-only. Delete spiders or subscribe to Pro to unlock them.

tarantula.lockedEditHint: Read-only on the free plan — upgrade to Pro or delete spiders to unlock editing.
tarantula.freePlanLockedBanner: This spider is read-only on the free plan. It stays visible — to edit it, free a slot or upgrade to Pro.
tarantula.historyEmpty: No events recorded yet. Start by logging a feeding or molt.
tarantula.notesTabEmpty: No keeper notes yet. You can add them when editing this spider.
tarantula.remindersForSpiderEmpty: No reminders for this spider yet. Create one from All reminders.
tarantula.remindersSignInHint: Sign in to see reminders for this spider.
tarantula.qrPrivateHelpPro: This specimen is still private for visitors. Turn on "Make public" on its page so anyone can open this card from the QR without logging in.
tarantula.qrPrivateHelpFree: Visitors only see a public card when you enable it — that option is included with Pro. Upgrade (or use your trial) to publish the card and unlock quick logging from the QR.
tarantula.deceasedDesc: This will stay in {{name}}'s file. You can still view their history.

species.estimatedNote: * Care parameters based on keeper literature and community guides.
species.estimatedNoteWsc: Taxonomy verified by World Spider Catalog.
species.hobbyWorldInferenceNote: Inferred from native-range text in our catalog and, when needed, GBIF distribution hints. Edge cases or outdated records can be wrong.
species.hobbyWorldNwContext: Hobby context: many New World species are discussed together with urticating hairs, but presence and severity vary by species and individual—not every New World spider behaves the same.
species.hobbyWorldOwContext: Hobby context: Old World species are often described as faster or more defensive biters, but behavior varies widely; some individuals are calm and some New World species are very defensive too.
species.hobbyWorldContextDisclaimer: Educational generalization only—not species-specific facts, medical advice, or a substitute for your own research and safe handling.
species.hobbyWorldSnippetNote: † New/Old World from range data (best-effort). See the full species profile for longer context.
species.careMoltFallbackBody: Molt timing varies with species, age, sex, feeding, temperature, and humidity. Your own logs in TarantulApp are the best way to see trends—avoid judging from a single event.

terrarium.title: 🏠 Terrarium recommendation *
terrarium.estimatedNote: * Estimated based on the species' adult size and keeper guides.

form.name: Name *
form.photoCropHint: You can adjust the crop before saving.
form.limitReached: You've reached the free plan limit of 6 tarantulas.
form.limitTrialHint: Pro gives you an unlimited collection. New accounts get a 7-day trial; if you already used yours, subscribe to continue.
form.importError: Could not import species from GBIF.
form.importErrorWsc: Could not import species from WSC.
form.postCreateDesc: Do you want to log the last feeding or last molt now so tracking starts with better data?
form.postCreateSaveError: Could not save the initial record.
form.firstFeedingHint: If you don't remember the date, that's fine: the feeding schedule can start when you log the first real feeding.

reminders.panelProNotice: Includes manual reminders and Pro automatic suggestions.
reminders.freeNotice: Free plan: manual reminders only (ones you create yourself).
reminders.freeAutoUpsell: Pro adds automatic feeding reminders based on each spider’s history. Try Pro or upgrade to turn them on.
reminders.panelFreeUpsell: Want automatic feeding reminders? That’s included in Pro.
reminders.proNotice: Pro plan active: you see manual reminders and automatic feeding suggestions.
reminders.prefillReady: New reminder: {{name}} is pre-selected. Choose date and time, then save.
reminders.prefillLocked: {{name}} is read-only on your plan — pick another spider or upgrade to Pro.
reminders.prefillNotFound: That spider is not in your collection (or was removed). Choose a spider manually.

gallery.title: 📷 Gallery
gallery.empty: No photos in the gallery yet.

logModals.publishToFeed: Publish this event on my community feed

public.notFound: Profile not found or not public.
public.privateLoginHint: If this is your spider, sign in to open its card from this link.
public.ownerPrivateBannerPro: Others still see a lock screen. Turn on "Make public" on the specimen page so any device can open this card from the QR.
public.ownerPrivateBannerFree: A public card for visitors (no login) is included with Pro. Upgrade to publish this specimen and unlock quick logging from the QR.
public.freeOwnerQrReadOnlyBody: Here you can see the card and history. To add feedings, molts, or behaviors, open this specimen from your collection (the free plan does not allow logging changes from the QR view alone).
public.loginToAccess: Sign in to view your collection
public.quickActionsProCTA: To log feedings, molts and behaviors when someone scans your QR you need Pro. New accounts get a 7-day free trial with everything unlocked.
public.keeperNotFound: We could not find this keeper.
public.keeperNoBio: This keeper has not added a public bio yet.
public.keeperSeoDescription: Keeper card, badges, and public collection on {{brand}}.
public.keeperCollectionPrivate: This keeper’s collection is private. Only public specimen cards they choose to publish appear here when collection sharing is on.
public.keeperCollectionEmpty: No public specimens yet. They may still be private per spider.

pro.title: TarantulApp™ plans
pro.subtitle: Subscription-focused: Pro for active keepers and Vendor/Business for commercial operations.
pro.planMatrixIntro: Founder Seller is an internal invitation-only program and is not part of the public offer. This page only shows subscription plans.
pro.trialThenPricing: 7-day trial on signup, then Pro for $4.99/month or $49.99/year (~17% less than paying month-by-month).
pro.pricingAfterTrial: After the trial: Pro from $4.99/month or $49.99/year — or stay on Free with the cap and core community features.
pro.tierFreeTagline: Wide funnel: no card required to vote, post, and browse.
pro.tierFreeLi1: Up to 6 editable spiders (extras become read-only until you upgrade or free a slot).
pro.tierFreeLi3: Community: emotional posts and keeper conversation.
pro.tierFreeLi5: Basic AI: general answers without advanced scoring panels.
pro.tierProTagline: For serious hobbyists: full logbook, exports, and bulk QR.
pro.tierProLi1: Unlimited spiders and full history (logs + insights).
pro.tierProLi2: Exports: Excel, PDF, and collection reports.
pro.tierProLi3: Bulk QR (Word / batch) plus quick actions per spider.
pro.tierProLi4: Deeper AI: longer explanations and finer scoring.
pro.tierProLi5: Cases: full aggregated analysis (more votes = stronger signal).
pro.tierProLi6: Pro analytics: molt patterns, feeding gaps, and collection rhythm.
pro.tierVendorTagline: Commercial layer for shops and breeders: storefront, verification, and performance tooling.
pro.tierVendorLi1: Verified profile + badge in community and marketplace.
pro.tierVendorLi2: Featured listings + mini storefront inside marketplace.
pro.tierVendorLi3: Lead analytics (clicks, origin, listings that convert).
pro.sellerTypesIntro: We define three profiles to avoid policy overlap: Founder (strategic program), Business (vendor subscription), and Community (peer listings).
pro.founderSellerTagline: Curated founding partners selected for quality, traceability, and trust signal.
pro.founderSellerLi1: Founder badge + priority placement among official vendors.
pro.founderSellerLi2: Assisted catalog import and launch support with the team.
pro.founderSellerLi3: Visibility perks and co-marketing during the founder program window.
pro.founderSellerLi4: Does not replace Business subscription: it is a strategic layer with entry criteria.
pro.businessSellerTagline: Shops and breeders with recurring operations that need stable commercial presence.
pro.businessSellerLi1: Business badge + storefront and prioritized commercial listings.
pro.businessSellerLi2: Lead metrics and listing-level performance traceability.
pro.businessSellerLi3: Operational support and compliance policies for responsible selling.
pro.businessSellerLi4: Subscription + KYC/compliance review required (no founder perks by default).
pro.communitySellerTagline: Community keepers posting occasional listings without intensive commercial tooling.
pro.communitySellerLi1: Publish standard listings with organic marketplace reach.
pro.communitySellerLi2: No commercial badge or paid-priority distribution.
pro.communitySellerLi3: Governed by animal welfare, listing accuracy, and community reputation rules.
pro.communitySellerLi4: Can upgrade to Business when they need volume/commercial tools.
pro.sellerPolicyFootnote: Policy framework: Founder = curated program; Business = operational subscription; Community = open participation. Distinct benefits prevent value collision.
pro.businessRolloutTitle: Vendor/Business rollout: scope and limits (current phase)
pro.businessRolloutIntro: We are preparing subscription activation by phases to protect marketplace quality and compliance.
pro.businessRolloutLi2: Phase 2: badge/storefront activation + first commercial listings.
pro.businessRolloutLi3: Phase 3: public Vendor/Business checkout with consolidated policies.
pro.businessRolloutLi4: Founder keeps strategic perks; Business receives scalable operational benefits.
pro.businessWaitlistValidation: Complete business, contact, and email to continue.
pro.businessWaitlistSuccess: Request submitted. We will contact you for Business onboarding.
pro.businessWaitlistError: We could not save your request right now. Please try again in a few minutes.
pro.referralsKeyline: Invite other keepers — improve your results.
pro.referralsIntro: Milestone ladder (one-time bonus when you cross each tier, on top of +3/+3 per signup):
pro.referralsBase: Each valid invite: +3 days for your friend on signup and +3 days for you (stacked onto the active trial).
pro.statusFree: Free: great for Cases, Community, and Discover without paying.
pro.statusPro: Pro is active. Vendor/Business rolls out in phases for commercial accounts.
pro.priceYearlyNote: Annual billing via Stripe once the yearly price id is configured in Railway.
pro.checkoutSuccessBody: Your plan is upgraded. Pro features are active; Vendor/Business arrives in the next phase.
pro.checkoutSuccess: Payment completed. We are updating your Pro plan.
pro.checkoutCancel: Checkout cancelled. Your current plan did not change.
pro.checkoutError: Could not start checkout right now.
pro.checkoutNotConfigured: Stripe checkout is not configured in this environment yet.
pro.androidPurchaseUnavailable: In-app Pro purchases are temporarily unavailable on Android while we finish Google Play Billing integration.
pro.androidPlaceholderHelp: Temporary developer flow: paste a test purchase token (for now use values starting with test_, sandbox_, or fake_) and sync it to your account.
pro.androidPurchaseSynced: Android purchase synced. Your plan is now Pro.
pro.androidPurchasePending: Purchase received but still pending confirmation.
pro.publicToggleProOnlyHint: Upgrade to Pro to make this card scannable by anyone.
pro.ctaRegisterTrialReminder: New accounts: 7-day trial with full Pro. After that pick Free or Pro; referrals add short trial days and milestones without replacing paid Pro.

contact.title: Contact
contact.intro: Pick the inbox that best matches your message so we can reply faster.
contact.helloBody: Questions about the app, your account, billing in general, or anything that is not strictly technical.
contact.supportBody: Bugs, something broken, odd data, or if you spotted a problem: tell us what happened and, if you can, steps to reproduce.
contact.billingBody: Pro payments, renewals, invoices, refunds, or charge issues related to Stripe.
contact.marketplaceBody: Questions about listings, seller profiles, official partners, or marketplace-related reports.
contact.communityBody: Content reports, abuse/spam, visibility problems, or community feature issues. Messaging is handled in marketplace.
contact.newsletterBody: Newsletter opt-in/opt-out, deliverability, subscriber campaigns, and lifecycle email topics.
contact.trustBody: Fraud, impersonation, security risks, animal welfare concerns, or urgent review requests.
contact.legalBody: Terms, privacy, data rights, deletion requests, and contract-related topics.
contact.partnersBody: Commercial partnerships, vendors, business integrations, and strategic opportunities.
contact.platformOpsTitle: Platform Ops — Google Play, Apple, Stripe and platforms
contact.platformOpsBody: Operational communication with Google Play Console, App Store Connect, Stripe, or other providers.
contact.marketingBody: Sponsorships, collaborations, press, or commercial ideas you would like to explore with TarantulApp™.

admin.title: Admin panel
admin.generalPageBlurb: SMTP, KPIs, users, marketplace moderation, and vendor tools.
admin.betaPageBlurb: Applications, testers, welcome templates, campaigns, and beta bug queue.
admin.recentUsersBlurb: Who last used the app (activity) or newest signups (joined). Badges: Active = used app in the last 7 days; Seen before = older activity; Not seen = no recorded session yet.
admin.recentUsersShowing: Showing {{count}} of {{total}} users (this request returns up to {{limit}}). If the count does not grow when you raise the limit, there are no more users in the database.
admin.strategicPartnerSectionBlurb: Enable founder program + listing import per vendor, then run a manual sync (uses static JSON or mock adapters until real feeds exist). See docs/operations/strategic-partner-day-zero-playbook.md.
admin.betaBugScreenshotHint: Upload to Imgur, Google Drive, etc. and paste the link. We already capture the current screen URL, viewport, and app version when you submit.
admin.addTesterBlurb: Add or upgrade someone to beta tester. Use their email, or their public @handle to find an existing account. Login always uses email + password. New accounts are only created when the identifier is an email and no user exists yet.
admin.identifierHint: Existing user: same email as login, or @handle. New user: use email only.
admin.passwordOrGenerate: Set a password, or use generate
admin.betaEmailTemplatesBlurb: Built-in templates match the repo welcome emails (ES/EN). Add custom bodies with placeholders: {{name}}, {{email}}, {{password}}, {{appUrl}}, {{sendDate}}.
admin.betaEmailTemplatesHint: Placeholders are replaced when you copy or export Word. The signature image is appended in .docx exports.
admin.betaEmailTemplatesResetConfirm: Discard all custom templates and restore built-in welcome templates?
admin.mailSmtpCardBlurb: Password reset, trial emails, and admin notifications use these settings. See docs/operations/smtp-ionos-railway.md for IONOS on Railway.
admin.mailSmtpMissingCreds: MAIL_USERNAME and MAIL_PASSWORD are not set in the server environment — no email will be sent. Add them in Railway and redeploy.
admin.mailTestSent: Test email sent. Check inbox and spam.
admin.mailTestFailed: Test send failed. Check server logs and SMTP variables.
admin.welcomeEmailPasswordPrompt: Password to embed (leave empty for placeholder):
admin.welcomeEmailCopyFailed: Could not copy — select and copy manually.
admin.testerUpdated: Tester access updated. Share the email and (if you set a password) the password with them. Login is always with email, not the public handle.
admin.devicesPlaceholder: Rough idea is fine — e.g. iPhone, Android, tablet, or desktop OS (helps us target builds, not collect hardware IDs).
admin.betaApplyLead: Fill this form and we will review your profile for the next test cohort.
admin.betaApplyHeadline: Help shape the platform that will change how hobbyists manage their tarantulas.
admin.betaApplySubLead: Be part of the first wave of testers building the most complete collection-management platform our community has ever had.
admin.betaApplyEligibility: Now accepting applications from 🇺🇸 USA, 🇲🇽 Mexico and 🇨🇦 Canada.
admin.betaApplyFeedbackPledge: Your feedback will directly shape the biggest, most complete platform the tarantula community has ever had.
admin.betaApplyPerksLine: Free Pro time · exclusive perks · early-tester gifts
admin.betaApplyAnyLevel: No coding skills needed — if you love tarantulas, you’re in. Any level welcome.
admin.betaApplySubmitted: We received your application. We will review it and contact you by email soon.
admin.betaApplySubmittedSubtitle: Keep an eye on your inbox — we will reach out to you here in the next days.
admin.betaApplyTimeout: Request timed out. Please try again in a few seconds.
admin.approveSendWelcomeLabel: Send the automated welcome email when approving (includes login details when a new password is generated).
admin.approveSendWelcomeHint: If the account already existed and no new password was generated, sending is skipped automatically.
admin.provisionWelcomeModalBlurb: A random password was generated. Send the welcome email now so they receive login instructions, or skip and share credentials another way.
admin.provisionCreatedChooseWelcome: Tester saved. Choose whether to send the welcome email below.
admin.campaignEmailBlurb: Pick testers and send this week's mission when you're ready. Body text is defined on the server (weeks 1–6); the table shows each person's last send per campaign.
admin.campaignSendPartial: Sent {{sent}}; some failed — check the response or logs.
admin.welcomeEmailSmtpFailed: Approval saved, but welcome email failed: {{detail}}
admin.welcomeSkippedNoPassword: (Welcome email not sent: approval must generate a new password.)

share.indicatorsSubtitle: Public profile, link, and edit limits for your plan
share.speciesBlockSubtitle: From the catalog; tune to your enclosure and this spider
share.notEnoughData: Need at least 2 feedings to compute a mean
share.snapshotSubtitle: Core data for registry and handling
share.pedigreeNote: Update this PDF as you add pairings and genealogy data.

onboarding.guideIntro: Guided checklist to set up your first specimen properly.
onboarding.setupArboreal: Suggested setup: more height and anchor points (arboreal)
onboarding.setupFossorial: Suggested setup: deeper substrate for burrowing (fossorial)
onboarding.setupTerrestrial: Suggested setup: wider floor space and low height (terrestrial)
onboarding.postCreateHint: After create: add last feeding or molt for better tracking

ratePrompt.title: Enjoying TarantulApp™?
ratePrompt.body: If TarantulApp™ is helping with your collection, would you like to leave a quick rating on Google Play?

profile.handleAvailabilityInvalid: Use 3–60 characters: letters, numbers, dot, underscore, or hyphen.
profile.locationPlaceholder: City or region (shown if you publish on the marketplace)
profile.featuredCollectionPlaceholder: Line or specialty you want to highlight
profile.searchVisibleLabel: Appear in public keeper search results
profile.collectionPublicLabel: Show my public collection on my keeper profile

billing.cancelScheduled: Your subscription is set to cancel at the end of the billing period.
billing.portalUnavailable: No billing customer linked yet. If you just paid, wait a few minutes or contact support.

password.mismatch: Confirmation does not match the new password.

errors.generic: Could not complete the action. Try again.
errors.PASSWORD_LENGTH: Password must be between 6 and 100 characters.
errors.SAME_PASSWORD: New password must be different from the current one.
errors.STRIPE_NOT_CONFIGURED: Payments are not configured in this environment.
errors.NO_STRIPE_CUSTOMER: No Stripe billing data for this account.
errors.PORTAL_FAILED: Could not open the billing portal.

help.noEmail: Set VITE_SUPPORT_EMAIL in the frontend environment to show a support address.
